visual_notes: Aventurescence in sunstone appears as a glittering, spangling metallic flash — brighter and more dramatic than the soft glow of adularescence in moonstone. Oregon sunstone is the only known feldspar with native copper inclusions, giving it a uniquely warm, saturated color. The metallic sparkle in Norwegian sunstone has a coppery-gold quality.
prompt_fragments: warm copper-orange metallic glitter in transparent feldspar
Oregon native copper feldspar with deep bicolor red-green pleochroism
vibe_notes: Sunstone has a grounded, warm, solar quality that contrasts with the cool lunar depth of moonstone. The aventurescent glitter reads as scattered light, like sunlight on moving water or the scatter of a copper fire. Oregon sunstone, with its native copper and rich saturated color, is among the most texturally and chromatically complex feldspars.
folklore_summary: The term 'sólarsteinn' (sunstone) from Norse sagas refers to a navigational crystal, which has generated scholarly debate.
